key_insight: A fine-tuned interpretable vision transformer can classify fracture causes in zirconia-toughened alumina implants, and low-magnification SEM images alone can be nearly as informative as high-magnification views.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost pre-screening in ceramic implant quality assurance while supporting more consistent fracture-origin analysis for patient safety.
method: Curated 8,493 SEM images spanning 50x–10,000x from five years of burst and proof tests.
Annotated fractures into three manufacturing-linked defect classes: green body, hard machining, and material defects.
Trained and validated an interpretable ViT with five-fold stratified cross-validation plus leakage auditing using perceptual hash and SSIM checks.
evidence_signal: The model achieved 0.907 accuracy and 0.888 macro-F1, with low-magnification (50x) performance comparable to 1k–10kx images.
watch_item: Performance was demonstrated on a single implant material system and depends on careful control of specimen leakage and annotation quality.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
